title: Where and What: Reasoning Dynamic and Implicit Preferences in Situated Conversational Recommendation
summary: Situated conversational recommendation (SCR), which utilizes visual scenes grounded in specific environments and natural language dialogue to deliver contextually appropriate recommendations, has emerged as a promising research direction due to its close alignment with real-world scenarios. Compared to traditional recommendations, SCR requires a deeper understanding of dynamic and implicit user preferences, as the surrounding scene often influences users' underlying interests, while both may evolve across conversations. This complexity significantly impacts the timing and relevance of recommendations. To address this, we propose situated preference reasoning (SiPeR), a novel framework that integrates two core mechanisms: (1) Scene transition estimation, which estimates whether the current scene satisfies user needs, and guides the user toward a more suitable scene when necessary; and (2) Bayesian inverse inference, which leverages the likelihood of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to predict user preferences about candidate items within the scene. Extensive experiments on two representative benchmarks demonstrate SiPeR's superiority in both recommendation accuracy and response generation quality. The code and data are available at https://github.com/DongdingLin/SiPeR.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为SiPeR的情境偏好推理框架，用于解决情境对话推荐中的动态偏好理解问题，该框架通过场景转换估计和贝叶斯逆推理机制，显著提升了推荐准确性和响应生成质量。
AI.motivation: 情境对话推荐相比传统推荐需要更深入地理解动态且隐式的用户偏好，因为周围场景会影响用户的潜在兴趣，且两者都可能随对话演变，这种复杂性显著影响了推荐的时机和相关性，现有方法难以有效处理这一挑战。
AI.method: 本文提出SiPeR框架，包含两个核心机制：场景转换估计用于判断当前场景是否满足用户需求并在必要时引导用户转向更合适的场景；贝叶斯逆推理则利用多模态大语言模型的似然度来预测用户在场景内对候选项目的偏好。
AI.result: 在两个代表性基准测试上的广泛实验表明，SiPeR在推荐准确性和响应生成质量方面均表现出优越性，具体体现在多个评估指标上的显著提升，验证了所提框架在情境对话推荐任务中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究证明了整合场景动态理解和贝叶斯推理机制对于情境对话推荐的重要性，为处理动态用户偏好和场景演变提供了新思路，未来可进一步探索更复杂的多模态交互和长期偏好建模方法。

title: V-tableR1: Process-Supervised Multimodal Table Reasoning with Critic-Guided Policy Optimization
summary: We introduce V-tableR1, a process-supervised reinforcement learning framework that elicits rigorous, verifiable reasoning from multimodal large language models (MLLMs). Current MLLMs trained solely on final outcomes often treat visual reasoning as a black box, relying on superficial pattern matching rather than performing rigorous multi-step inference. While Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards could enforce transparent reasoning trajectories, extending it to visual domains remains severely hindered by the ambiguity of grounding abstract logic into continuous pixel space. We solve this by leveraging the deterministic grid structure of tables as an ideal visual testbed. V-tableR1 employs a specialized critic VLM to provide dense, step-level feedback on the explicit visual chain-of-thought generated by a policy VLM. To optimize this system, we propose Process-Guided Direct Alignment Policy Optimization (PGPO), a novel RL algorithm integrating process rewards, decoupled policy constraints, and length-aware dynamic sampling. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that V-tableR1 explicitly penalizes visual hallucinations and shortcut guessing. By fundamentally shifting multimodal inference from black-box pattern matching to verifiable logical derivation, V-tableR1 4B establishes state-of-the-art accuracy among open-source models on complex tabular benchmarks, outperforming models up to 18x its size and improving over its SFT baseline

AI.tldr: 本文提出V-tableR1，一种基于过程监督强化学习的框架，旨在从多模态大语言模型中引出严谨、可验证的推理过程。该框架通过专门的批评者视觉语言模型提供密集的步骤级反馈，并结合新颖的PGPO算法，在表格推理任务上实现了最先进的性能。
AI.motivation: 当前多模态大语言模型仅基于最终结果进行训练，将视觉推理视为黑箱，依赖表面的模式匹配而非严谨的多步推理。虽然可验证奖励的强化学习可以强制执行透明的推理轨迹，但将其扩展到视觉领域面临将抽象逻辑映射到连续像素空间的模糊性挑战。
AI.method: V-tableR1利用表格的确定性网格结构作为理想的视觉测试平台，采用专门的批评者视觉语言模型为策略视觉语言模型生成的显式视觉思维链提供密集的步骤级反馈。为优化该系统，提出了过程引导直接对齐策略优化算法，该算法整合了过程奖励、解耦策略约束和长度感知动态采样。
AI.result: 广泛评估表明，V-tableR1能显式惩罚视觉幻觉和捷径猜测。在复杂的表格基准测试中，4B参数的V-tableR1在开源模型中建立了最先进的准确率，性能超过其规模18倍的模型，并显著优于其监督微调基线。
AI.conclusion: 该研究从根本上将多模态推理从黑箱模式匹配转变为可验证的逻辑推导，为视觉推理的透明性和可解释性提供了新范式。通过过程监督强化学习框架，实现了对推理过程的显式控制和质量保证，为复杂视觉推理任务提供了有效的解决方案。

title: If you're waiting for a sign... that might not be it! Mitigating Trust Boundary Confusion from Visual Injections on Vision-Language Agentic Systems
summary: Recent advances in embodied Vision-Language Agentic Systems (VLAS), powered by large vision-language models (LVLMs), enable AI systems to perceive and reason over real-world scenes. Within this context, environmental signals such as traffic lights are essential in-band signals that can and should influence agent behavior. However, similar signals could also be crafted to operate as misleading visual injections, overriding user intent and posing security risks. This duality creates a fundamental challenge: agents must respond to legitimate environmental cues while remaining robust to misleading ones. We refer to this tension as trust boundary confusion. To study this behavior, we design a dual-intent dataset and evaluation framework, through which we show that current LVLM-based agents fail to reliably balance this trade-off, either ignoring useful signals or following harmful ones. We systematically evaluate 7 LVLM agents across multiple embodied settings under both structure-based and noise-based visual injections. To address these vulnerabilities, we propose a multi-agent defense framework that separates perception from decision-making to dynamically assess the reliability of visual inputs. Our approach significantly reduces misleading behaviors while preserving correct responses and provides robustness guarantees under adversarial perturbations. The code of the evaluation framework and artifacts are made available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Visual-Prompt-Inject.

AI.tldr: 该研究揭示了基于大视觉语言模型的具身智能体面临信任边界混淆的安全风险，即无法可靠区分环境中的合法信号与恶意视觉注入，并提出了一个多智能体防御框架来增强系统鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 基于大视觉语言模型的具身视觉语言智能体系统在感知和推理真实世界场景时，面临环境信号的双重性挑战：交通灯等合法信号应指导智能体行为，但类似信号也可被精心设计为误导性视觉注入，从而覆盖用户意图并带来安全风险。这种信任边界混淆问题导致智能体难以平衡对有用信号的响应与对有害信号的抵抗，当前系统在此方面存在明显缺陷。
AI.method: 研究设计了一个双意图数据集和评估框架来系统研究信任边界混淆行为，对7种大视觉语言模型智能体在多种具身场景下进行了评估，涵盖基于结构和基于噪声的视觉注入攻击。为应对这些漏洞，提出了一个多智能体防御框架，将感知与决策分离以动态评估视觉输入的可靠性，该框架能在对抗扰动下提供鲁棒性保证。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，当前基于大视觉语言模型的智能体无法可靠平衡这一权衡，要么忽略有用信号，要么跟随有害信号。提出的多智能体防御框架显著减少了误导行为，同时保持了正确响应，在对抗扰动下提供了鲁棒性保证。评估框架和实验工件已开源提供。
AI.conclusion: 该研究揭示了具身视觉语言智能体系统中信任边界混淆的根本安全挑战，表明当前系统在区分合法环境信号与恶意视觉注入方面存在严重不足。提出的多智能体防御框架通过分离感知与决策层，为增强系统安全性和鲁棒性提供了有效解决方案，对实际部署中的安全考虑具有重要指导意义。

title: Infection-Reasoner: A Compact Vision-Language Model for Wound Infection Classification with Evidence-Grounded Clinical Reasoning
summary: Assessing chronic wound infection from photographs is challenging because visual appearance varies across wound etiologies, anatomical locations, and imaging conditions. Prior image-based deep learning methods have mainly focused on classification with limited interpretability, despite the need for evidence-grounded explanations to support point-of-care decision making. We present Infection-Reasoner, a compact 4B-parameter reasoning vision-language model for chronic wound infection classification and rationale generation. To address the scarcity of expert-labeled wound images with reasoning annotations, Infection-Reasoner is trained using a two-stage pipeline: (1) reasoning distillation, in which GPT-5.1 generates chain-of-thought rationales for unlabeled wound images to initialize wound-specific reasoning in a smaller student model (Qwen3-VL-4B-Thinking), and (2) reinforcement learning post-training with Group Relative Policy Optimization on a small labeled infection dataset to refine classification reasoning. On a held-out heterogeneous wound dataset, Infection-Reasoner achieved 86.8\% accuracy, 86.4\% sensitivity, and 87.1\% specificity, outperforming several strong baselines, including GPT-5.1. Rationale quality was further evaluated using both multimodal large language model (MLLM) judges and wound expert review. Across four MLLM judges, visual-support agreement scores ranged from 0.722 to 0.903, while expert review rated 61.8\% of rationales as Correct and 32.4\% as Partially Correct.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出了Infection-Reasoner，一个紧凑的40亿参数推理视觉语言模型，用于慢性伤口感染分类和原理生成，通过两阶段训练管道解决了伤口图像标注稀缺的问题，并在异质伤口数据集上超越了包括GPT-5.1在内的多个强基线。
AI.motivation: 慢性伤口感染的视觉评估具有挑战性，因为伤口外观因病因、解剖位置和成像条件而异，而现有的基于图像的深度学习方法主要关注分类任务且可解释性有限，无法为临床决策提供基于证据的解释支持。
AI.method: 本研究提出了Infection-Reasoner，一个紧凑的40亿参数推理视觉语言模型，采用两阶段训练管道：首先通过推理蒸馏，利用GPT-5.1为未标注伤口图像生成思维链原理，在较小的学生模型（Qwen3-VL-4B-Thinking）中初始化伤口特定推理能力；然后在小规模标注感染数据集上使用组相对策略优化进行强化学习后训练，以精化分类推理过程。
AI.result: 在异质伤口测试集上，Infection-Reasoner达到了86.8%的准确率、86.4%的敏感性和87.1%的特异性，超越了包括GPT-5.1在内的多个强基线。原理质量评估显示，四个多模态大语言模型评委的视觉支持一致性得分在0.722至0.903之间，而专家评审认为61.8%的原理完全正确，32.4%部分正确。
AI.conclusion: 该研究表明，通过推理蒸馏和强化学习后训练的组合策略，可以在专家标注稀缺的情况下构建高性能的医疗视觉推理模型，为临床决策提供可解释的感染评估支持，展示了紧凑模型在专业医疗任务中超越大型通用模型的潜力。

title: AFMRL: Attribute-Enhanced Fine-Grained Multi-Modal Representation Learning in E-commerce
summary: Multimodal representation is crucial for E-commerce tasks such as identical product retrieval. Large representation models (e.g., VLM2Vec) demonstrate strong multimodal understanding capabilities, yet they struggle with fine-grained semantic comprehension, which is essential for distinguishing highly similar items. To address this, we propose Attribute-Enhanced Fine-Grained Multi-Modal Representation Learning (AFMRL), which defines product fine-grained understanding as an attribute generation task. It leverages the generative power of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to extract key attributes from product images and text, and enhances representation learning through a two-stage training framework: 1) Attribute-Guided Contrastive Learning (AGCL), where the key attributes generated by the MLLM are used in the image-text contrastive learning training process to identify hard samples and filter out noisy false negatives. 2) Retrieval-aware Attribute Reinforcement (RAR), where the improved retrieval performance of the representation model post-attribute integration serves as a reward signal to enhance MLLM's attribute generation during multimodal fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on large-scale E-commerce datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple downstream retrieval tasks, validating the effectiveness of harnessing generative models to advance fine-grained representation learning.

AI.tldr: 本文提出AFMRL方法，通过将商品细粒度理解定义为属性生成任务，利用多模态大语言模型的生成能力提取关键属性，并采用两阶段训练框架增强多模态表示学习，在电商检索任务中实现最先进性能。
AI.motivation: 现有大型表示模型（如VLM2Vec）在多模态理解方面表现出色，但在细粒度语义理解方面存在不足，难以区分高度相似的商品，而这对电商任务（如相同商品检索）至关重要。
AI.method: 提出属性增强细粒度多模态表示学习（AFMRL），将商品细粒度理解定义为属性生成任务，利用多模态大语言模型从商品图像和文本中提取关键属性，采用两阶段训练框架：1）属性引导对比学习，使用MLLM生成的关键属性在图像-文本对比学习训练过程中识别困难样本并过滤噪声假负例；2）检索感知属性强化，将属性集成后表示模型的检索性能改进作为奖励信号，在多模态微调中增强MLLM的属性生成能力。
AI.result: 在大规模电商数据集上的广泛实验表明，该方法在多个下游检索任务中实现了最先进的性能，验证了利用生成模型推进细粒度表示学习的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究展示了将生成模型能力与表示学习相结合的有效性，通过属性生成任务增强细粒度理解，为多模态表示学习提供了新范式，表明生成模型可以作为强大的语义提取器来增强判别式表示模型的性能。

title: Evian: Towards Explainable Visual Instruction-tuning Data Auditing
summary: The efficacy of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) is critically dependent on the quality of their training data, requiring a precise balance between visual fidelity and instruction-following capability. Existing datasets, however, are plagued by inconsistent quality, and current data filtering methods rely on coarse-grained scores that lack the granularity to identify nuanced semantic flaws like logical fallacies or factual errors. This creates a fundamental bottleneck in developing more reliable models. To address this, we make three core contributions. First, we construct a large-scale, 300K-sample benchmark by systematically injecting diverse, subtle defects to provide a challenging testbed for data auditing. Second, we introduce a novel "Decomposition-then-Evaluation" paradigm that breaks model responses into constituent cognitive components: visual description, subjective inference, and factual claim, enabling targeted analysis. Third, we instantiate this paradigm via EVIAN (Explainable Visual Instruction-tuning Data AuditiNg), an automated framework that evaluates these components along the orthogonal axes of Image-Text Consistency, Logical Coherence, and Factual Accuracy. Our empirical findings challenge the prevailing scale-centric paradigm: a model fine-tuned on a compact, high-quality subset curated by EVIAN consistently surpassed models trained on orders-of-magnitude larger datasets. We also reveal that dividing complex auditing into verifiable subtasks enables robust curation, and that Logical Coherence is the most critical factor in data quality evaluation.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新颖的"分解-评估"范式及其实例化框架EVIAN，用于细粒度审计视觉指令微调数据质量，挑战了当前以数据规模为中心的范式，证明高质量小数据集优于大规模低质量数据集。
AI.motivation: 大型视觉语言模型的效果严重依赖训练数据质量，但现有数据集存在质量不一致问题，且当前数据过滤方法依赖粗粒度评分，缺乏识别逻辑谬误或事实错误等细微语义缺陷的粒度，这成为开发更可靠模型的基本瓶颈。
AI.method: 研究提出三个核心贡献：首先构建包含30万样本的大规模基准数据集，通过系统注入多样细微缺陷提供数据审计的挑战性测试平台；其次引入新颖的"分解-评估"范式，将模型响应分解为视觉描述、主观推理和事实主张三个认知组件；最后实例化该范式为EVIAN框架，沿图像-文本一致性、逻辑连贯性和事实准确性三个正交轴评估这些组件。
AI.result: 实证研究结果挑战了当前以规模为中心的范式：使用EVIAN筛选的紧凑高质量子集微调的模型，在性能上持续超越基于数量级更大数据集训练的模型。研究还揭示将复杂审计分解为可验证子任务可实现稳健的数据筛选，并且逻辑连贯性是数据质量评估中最关键的因素。
AI.conclusion: 该研究提供了对视觉指令微调数据质量评估的新视角，表明数据质量而非数量是模型性能的关键决定因素。分解复杂审计任务为可验证子任务的方法为未来数据筛选技术提供了新方向，逻辑连贯性作为最关键评估维度的发现为数据质量评估提供了重要指导。

title: OMIBench: Benchmarking Olympiad-Level Multi-Image Reasoning in Large Vision-Language Model
summary: Large vision-language models (LVLMs) have made substantial advances in reasoning tasks at the Olympiad level. Nevertheless, current Olympiad-level multimodal reasoning benchmarks for these models often emphasize single-image analysis and fail to exploit contextual information across multiple images. We present OMIBench, a benchmark designed to evaluate Olympiad-level reasoning when the required evidence is distributed over multiple images. It contains problems from biology, chemistry, mathematics, and physics Olympiads, together with manually annotated rationales and evaluation protocols for both exact and semantic answer matching. Across extensive experiments on OMIBench, we observe meaningful performance gaps in existing models. Even the strongest LVLMs, such as Gemini-3-Pro, attain only about 50% on the benchmark. These results position OMIBench as a focused resources for studying and improving multi-image reasoning in LVLMs.

AI.tldr: 该研究提出了OMIBench基准测试，用于评估大型视觉语言模型在多图像推理任务中的表现，填补了现有奥林匹克级别多模态推理基准的空白，揭示了当前模型在多图像证据整合方面的显著性能差距。
AI.motivation: 当前奥林匹克级别的多模态推理基准主要关注单图像分析，未能充分利用多图像间的上下文信息，缺乏评估大型视觉语言模型在证据分布于多个图像时的推理能力的专门基准。
AI.method: 研究构建了OMIBench基准测试，包含来自生物、化学、数学和物理奥林匹克竞赛的问题，并提供了手动标注的推理过程和两种评估协议：精确答案匹配和语义答案匹配，专门设计用于评估多图像推理能力。
AI.result: 在OMIBench上的广泛实验显示，现有模型存在显著性能差距，即使是最强的LVLM如Gemini-3-Pro也仅能达到约50%的准确率，表明当前模型在多图像推理任务上仍有很大改进空间。
AI.conclusion: OMIBench为研究和改进大型视觉语言模型的多图像推理能力提供了聚焦资源，揭示了当前模型在整合分布式视觉证据方面的局限性，为未来模型开发指明了重要方向。

title: WildFireVQA: A Large-Scale Radiometric Thermal VQA Benchmark for Aerial Wildfire Monitoring
summary: Wildfire monitoring requires timely, actionable situational awareness from airborne platforms, yet existing aerial visual question answering (VQA) benchmarks do not evaluate wildfire-specific multimodal reasoning grounded in thermal measurements. We introduce WildFireVQA, a large-scale VQA benchmark for aerial wildfire monitoring that integrates RGB imagery with radiometric thermal data. WildFireVQA contains 6,097 RGB-thermal samples, where each sample includes an RGB image, a color-mapped thermal visualization, and a radiometric thermal TIFF, and is paired with 34 questions, yielding a total of 207,298 multiple-choice questions spanning presence and detection, classification, distribution and segmentation, localization and direction, cross-modal reasoning, and flight planning for operational wildfire intelligence. To improve annotation reliability, we combine multimodal large language model (MLLM)-based answer generation with sensor-driven deterministic labeling, manual verification, and intra-frame and inter-frame consistency checks. We further establish a comprehensive evaluation protocol for representative MLLMs under RGB, Thermal, and retrieval-augmented settings using radiometric thermal statistics. Experiments show that across task categories, RGB remains the strongest modality for current models, while retrieved thermal context yields gains for stronger MLLMs, highlighting both the value of temperature-grounded reasoning and the limitations of existing MLLMs in safety-critical wildfire scenarios. The dataset and benchmark code are open-source at https://github.com/mobiiin/WildFire_VQA.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了WildFireVQA，这是一个用于空中野火监测的大规模视觉问答基准，它整合了RGB图像与辐射热测量数据，旨在评估野火特定多模态推理能力。
AI.motivation: 现有空中视觉问答基准缺乏针对野火监测的多模态推理评估，特别是缺乏基于热测量数据的接地推理，这限制了在安全关键野火场景中及时获取可操作态势感知的能力。
AI.method: 研究团队构建了包含6,097个RGB-热样本的大规模数据集，每个样本包括RGB图像、彩色映射热可视化图像和辐射热TIFF文件，并配以34个问题，总计207,298个多项选择题。为提高标注可靠性，采用了基于多模态大语言模型的答案生成与传感器驱动确定性标注相结合的方法，辅以人工验证和帧内帧间一致性检查。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，在所有任务类别中，RGB仍然是当前模型最强的模态，而检索到的热上下文能为更强的大语言模型带来性能提升。这突显了基于温度接地推理的价值以及现有大语言模型在安全关键野火场景中的局限性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究强调了热测量数据在野火监测多模态推理中的重要性，揭示了现有模型在安全关键应用中的不足，并为开发更可靠的野火监测系统提供了基准和评估协议。开源的数据集和代码将促进该领域的研究进展。

title: UniCVR: From Alignment to Reranking for Unified Zero-Shot Composed Visual Retrieval
summary: Composed image retrieval, multi-turn composed image retrieval, and composed video retrieval all share a common paradigm: composing the reference visual with modification text to retrieve the desired target. Despite this shared structure, the three tasks have been studied in isolation, with no prior work proposing a unified framework, let alone a zero-shot solution. In this paper, we propose UniCVR, the first unified zero-shot composed visual retrieval framework that jointly addresses all three tasks without any task-specific human-annotated data. UniCVR strategically combines two complementary strengths: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for compositional query understanding and Vision-Language Pre-trained (VLP) models for structured visual retrieval. Concretely, UniCVR operates in two stages. In Stage I, we train the MLLM as a compositional query embedder via contrastive learning on a curated multi-source dataset of approximately 3.5M samples, bridging the heterogeneous embedding spaces between the MLLM and the frozen VLP gallery encoder. A cluster-based hard negative sampling strategy is proposed to strengthen contrastive supervision. In Stage II, we introduce an MLLM-guided dual-level reranking mechanism that applies adaptive budgeted subset scoring to a small number of top-ranked candidates, and then exploits the resulting relevance signals through a dual-level re-scoring scheme, producing more accurate final rankings with minimal computational overhead. Extensive experiments across five benchmarks covering all three tasks demonstrate that UniCVR achieves cutting-edge performance, validating its effectiveness and generalizability. Our data and code will be released upon acceptance.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了UniCVR，首个统一的零样本组合视觉检索框架，通过结合多模态大语言模型和视觉语言预训练模型的优势，在无需任务特定标注数据的情况下同时解决组合图像检索、多轮组合图像检索和组合视频检索三个任务。
AI.motivation: 尽管组合图像检索、多轮组合图像检索和组合视频检索共享相同的范式——通过组合参考视觉内容和修改文本来检索目标，但这些任务以往都是孤立研究的，缺乏统一的框架，更不用说零样本解决方案。本研究旨在填补这一空白，提出首个能够同时处理所有三个任务的统一零样本框架。
AI.method: UniCVR采用两阶段框架：第一阶段通过对比学习在多源数据集（约350万样本）上训练多模态大语言模型作为组合查询嵌入器，并采用基于聚类的困难负样本采样策略来增强对比监督；第二阶段引入MLLM引导的双级重排序机制，通过自适应预算子集评分对少量顶级候选进行评分，然后利用生成的相关信号通过双级重新评分方案产生更准确的最终排序。
AI.result: 在涵盖所有三个任务的五个基准测试上进行广泛实验，结果表明UniCVR实现了最先进的性能，验证了其有效性和泛化能力。该框架在无需任务特定人工标注数据的情况下，在组合视觉检索的多个任务上均表现出色。
AI.conclusion: UniCVR展示了统一框架在组合视觉检索任务中的可行性，通过战略性地结合MLLM的组合查询理解能力和VLP模型的结构化视觉检索优势，实现了零样本跨任务泛化。该研究为多模态检索系统提供了新的设计思路，表明通过精心设计的训练和重排序机制，可以在不依赖任务特定数据的情况下实现高性能。

title: SurgCoT: Advancing Spatiotemporal Reasoning in Surgical Videos through a Chain-of-Thought Benchmark
summary: Fine-grained spatiotemporal reasoning on surgical videos is critical, yet the capabilities of Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in this domain remain largely unexplored. To bridge this gap, we introduce SurgCoT, a unified benchmark for evaluating chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning in MLLMs across 7 surgical specialties and 35 diverse procedures. SurgCoT assesses five core reasoning dimensions: Causal Action Ordering, Cue-Action Alignment, Affordance Mapping, Micro-Transition Localization, and Anomaly Onset Tracking, through a structured CoT framework with an intensive annotation protocol (Question-Option-Knowledge-Clue-Answer), where the Knowledge field provides essential background context and Clue provides definitive spatiotemporal evidence. Evaluation of 10 leading MLLMs shows: 1) commercial models outperform open-source and medical-specialized variants; 2) significant gaps exist in surgical CoT reasoning; 3) SurgCoT enables effective evaluation and enhances progressive spatiotemporal reasoning. SurgCoT provides a reproducible testbed to narrow the gap between MLLM capabilities and clinical reasoning demands. Code: https://github.com/CVI-SZU/SurgCoT.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了SurgCoT，一个用于评估多模态大语言模型在手术视频中进行细粒度时空推理的统一基准，涵盖7个外科专业和35种不同手术程序，填补了该领域的研究空白。
AI.motivation: 当前多模态大语言模型在手术视频中进行细粒度时空推理的能力尚未得到充分探索，而这对临床决策至关重要。现有研究缺乏系统评估MLLMs在手术领域链式思维推理能力的统一基准，无法准确衡量模型在复杂临床推理任务中的表现。
AI.method: 研究提出了SurgCoT基准，采用结构化链式思维框架和密集标注协议（问题-选项-知识-线索-答案），其中知识字段提供背景上下文，线索字段提供确切的时空证据。该基准评估五个核心推理维度：因果动作排序、线索-动作对齐、可供性映射、微观转换定位和异常起始跟踪，覆盖7个外科专业和35种不同手术程序。
AI.result: 评估10个领先的MLLMs显示：1）商业模型优于开源和医学专用变体；2）在手术链式思维推理方面存在显著差距；3）SurgCoT能够有效评估并增强渐进式时空推理能力。基准提供了可复现的测试环境，能够准确衡量模型在复杂临床推理任务中的表现。
AI.conclusion: SurgCoT基准缩小了多模态大语言模型能力与临床推理需求之间的差距，为评估和提升模型在手术视频中的时空推理能力提供了标准化测试平台。该研究揭示了当前MLLMs在手术推理方面的局限性，并为未来开发更强大的医疗AI系统指明了方向。

title: Hybrid Latent Reasoning with Decoupled Policy Optimization
summary: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning significantly elevates the complex problem-solving capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs). However, adapting CoT to vision typically discretizes signals to fit LLM inputs, causing early semantic collapse and discarding fine-grained details. While external tools can mitigate this, they introduce a rigid bottleneck, confining reasoning to predefined operations. Although recent latent reasoning paradigms internalize visual states to overcome these limitations, optimizing the resulting hybrid discrete-continuous action space remains challenging. In this work, we propose HyLaR (Hybrid Latent Reasoning), a framework that seamlessly interleaves discrete text generation with continuous visual latent representations. Specifically, following an initial cold-start supervised fine-tuning (SFT), we introduce DePO (Decoupled Policy Optimization) to enable effective reinforcement learning within this hybrid space. DePO decomposes the policy gradient objective, applying independent trust-region constraints to the textual and latent components, alongside an exact closed-form von Mises-Fisher (vMF) KL regularizer. Extensive experiments demonstrate that HyLaR outperforms standard MLLMs and state-of-the-art latent reasoning approaches across fine-grained perception and general multimodal understanding benchmarks. Code is available at https://github.com/EthenCheng/HyLaR.

AI.tldr: 本文提出HyLaR（混合潜在推理）框架，通过无缝交织离散文本生成与连续视觉潜在表示来解决多模态大语言模型中链式思维推理的语义坍缩问题，并引入DePO（解耦策略优化）方法在混合空间中实现有效的强化学习。
AI.motivation: 传统链式思维推理在视觉任务中通常将信号离散化以适应LLM输入，导致早期语义坍并丢弃细粒度细节；虽然外部工具可以缓解此问题，但引入了刚性瓶颈，而现有潜在推理范式虽然内化了视觉状态，但在混合离散-连续动作空间的优化仍面临挑战。
AI.method: HyLaR框架无缝交织离散文本生成与连续视觉潜在表示，在初始冷启动监督微调后，引入DePO方法实现混合空间中的有效强化学习；DePO将策略梯度目标分解，对文本和潜在组件分别应用独立的信任域约束，并采用精确闭式von Mises-Fisher KL正则化器。
AI.result: 大量实验表明，HyLaR在细粒度感知和通用多模态理解基准测试中均优于标准MLLMs和最先进的潜在推理方法，证明了该方法在混合离散-连续动作空间优化方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究展示了混合潜在推理框架在解决多模态推理中语义坍缩问题的潜力，DePO方法为混合动作空间的强化学习优化提供了有效解决方案，为未来多模态推理系统的设计提供了新方向。

title: X-PCR: A Benchmark for Cross-modality Progressive Clinical Reasoning in Ophthalmic Diagnosis
summary: Despite significant progress in Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs), their clinical reasoning capacity for multi-modal diagnosis remains largely unexamined. Current benchmarks, mostly single-modality data, can't evaluate progressive reasoning and cross-modal integration essential for clinical practice. We introduce the Cross-Modality Progressive Clinical Reasoning (X-PCR) benchmark, the first comprehensive evaluation of MLLMs through a complete ophthalmology diagnostic workflow, with two reasoning tasks: 1) a six-stage progressive reasoning chain spanning image quality assessment to clinical decision-making, and 2) a cross-modality reasoning task integrating six imaging modalities. The benchmark comprises 26,415 images and 177,868 expert-verified VQA pairs curated from 51 public datasets, covering 52 ophthalmic diseases. Evaluation of 21 MLLMs reveals critical gaps in progressive reasoning and cross-modal integration. Dataset and code: https://github.com/CVI-SZU/X-PCR.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了首个跨模态渐进临床推理基准X-PCR，用于全面评估多模态大语言模型在完整眼科诊断流程中的临床推理能力，揭示了现有模型在渐进推理和跨模态整合方面的关键不足。
AI.motivation: 尽管多模态大语言模型取得了显著进展，但其在多模态诊断中的临床推理能力尚未得到充分检验。现有基准大多基于单模态数据，无法评估临床实践中至关重要的渐进推理和跨模态整合能力，这构成了当前研究的重要空白。
AI.method: 研究引入了跨模态渐进临床推理基准X-PCR，包含两个核心推理任务：六阶段渐进推理链（从图像质量评估到临床决策）和跨模态推理任务（整合六种成像模态）。该基准包含从51个公共数据集中整理的26,415张图像和177,868个专家验证的视觉问答对，涵盖52种眼科疾病。
AI.result: 对21个多模态大语言模型的评估揭示了在渐进推理和跨模态整合方面的关键差距。模型在完整临床工作流程中的表现表明，现有方法在模拟真实临床决策过程方面存在显著不足，特别是在跨模态信息融合和渐进推理链的连贯性方面。
AI.conclusion: 该研究强调了开发专门针对临床推理的多模态大语言模型的必要性，为未来医疗AI研究提供了重要的评估框架。X-PCR基准的建立不仅填补了临床多模态推理评估的空白，也为模型改进指明了方向，特别是需要增强模型在复杂临床工作流程中的渐进推理和跨模态整合能力。

title: Mitigating Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models without Performance Degradation
summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) exhibit powerful generative capabilities but frequently produce hallucinations that compromise output reliability. Fine-tuning on annotated data devoid of hallucinations offers the most direct solution, while its high computational cost motivates recent representation-based methods, which focus on mitigating hallucinatory components within hidden representations. Though efficient, we empirically observe that these methods degrade general generation capacity due to incomplete extraction of hallucination components and non-selective parameter updates. To address these limitations, we propose MPD, a dual-stage framework for mitigating hallucinations without performance degradation. Specifically, our MPD relies on two essential factors: (1) semantic-aware component disentanglement to extract pure hallucination components, and (2) interpretable parameter updates that selectively modify parameters most relevant to hallucination. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MPD achieves state-of-the-art performance, reducing hallucinations by 23.4\% while maintaining 97.4\% of general generative capability as evaluated on LLaVA-Bench and MME, with no additional computational cost.

AI.tldr: 本文提出MPD框架，一种双阶段方法，通过语义感知组件解耦和可解释参数更新，有效减少大视觉语言模型的幻觉生成，同时保持其通用生成能力。
AI.motivation: 大视觉语言模型虽然具备强大的生成能力，但经常产生幻觉输出，影响可靠性。基于标注数据的微调方法计算成本高，而现有的基于表示的方法虽然高效，但会因幻觉成分提取不完整和非选择性参数更新而降低模型的通用生成能力。
AI.method: 本文提出MPD双阶段框架，包含两个关键要素：语义感知组件解耦用于提取纯幻觉成分，以及可解释参数更新选择性地修改与幻觉最相关的参数，从而在不降低性能的情况下缓解幻觉问题。
AI.result: 在LLaVA-Bench和MME基准上的广泛实验表明，MPD实现了最先进的性能，将幻觉减少23.4%，同时保持97.4%的通用生成能力，且无需额外计算成本。
AI.conclusion: MPD框架通过精确的幻觉成分提取和选择性参数更新，有效解决了现有方法在幻觉缓解与生成能力保持之间的权衡问题，为大视觉语言模型的可靠性提升提供了高效且可扩展的解决方案。

title: R-CoV: Region-Aware Chain-of-Verification for Alleviating Object Hallucinations in LVLMs
summary: Large vision-language models (LVLMs) have demonstrated impressive performance in various multimodal understanding and reasoning tasks. However, they still struggle with object hallucinations, i.e., the claim of nonexistent objects in the visual input. To address this challenge, we propose Region-aware Chain-of-Verification (R-CoV), a visual chain-of-verification method to alleviate object hallucinations in LVLMs in a post-hoc manner. Motivated by how humans comprehend intricate visual information -- often focusing on specific image regions or details within a given sample -- we elicit such region-level processing from LVLMs themselves and use it as a chaining cue to detect and alleviate their own object hallucinations. Specifically, our R-CoV consists of six steps: initial response generation, entity extraction, coordinate generation, region description, verification execution, and final response generation. As a simple yet effective method, R-CoV can be seamlessly integrated into various LVLMs in a training-free manner and without relying on external detection models. Extensive experiments on several widely used hallucination benchmarks across multiple LVLMs demonstrate that R-CoV can significantly alleviate object hallucinations in LVLMs. Project page: https://github.com/Jiahao000/R-CoV.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种区域感知的验证链方法（R-CoV），通过后处理方式缓解大型视觉语言模型中的物体幻觉问题。该方法利用模型自身的区域级处理能力作为链式线索，以训练无关且无需外部检测模型的方式显著减少物体幻觉。
AI.motivation: 大型视觉语言模型在多种多模态理解和推理任务中表现出色，但仍面临物体幻觉的挑战，即在视觉输入中声称存在不存在的物体。现有方法难以有效解决这一问题，需要一种能够利用模型自身能力检测和缓解幻觉的后处理方案。
AI.method: 本文提出了区域感知的验证链方法，包含六个步骤：初始响应生成、实体提取、坐标生成、区域描述、验证执行和最终响应生成。该方法受人类理解复杂视觉信息的启发，通过引导模型进行区域级处理作为链式线索，以训练无关的方式集成到各种大型视觉语言模型中，且不依赖外部检测模型。
AI.result: 在多个广泛使用的幻觉基准测试和多种大型视觉语言模型上的大量实验表明，R-CoV方法能够显著缓解物体幻觉问题。该方法在多个基准测试中均表现出优越性能，验证了其作为简单而有效解决方案的有效性。
AI.conclusion: R-CoV提供了一种新颖的后处理框架，通过利用模型自身的区域处理能力来检测和纠正幻觉，为缓解大型视觉语言模型中的物体幻觉问题提供了有效途径。该方法具有训练无关、无需外部模型的优势，展示了自监督验证在提高模型可靠性方面的潜力。

title: SSL-R1: Self-Supervised Visual Reinforcement Post-Training for Multimodal Large Language Models
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has demonstrated the great potential of enhancing the reasoning abilities in multimodal large language models (MLLMs). However, the reliance on language-centric priors and expensive manual annotations prevents MLLMs' intrinsic visual understanding and scalable reward designs. In this work, we introduce SSL-R1, a generic self-supervised RL framework that derives verifiable rewards directly from images. To this end, we revisit self-supervised learning (SSL) in visual domains and reformulate widely-used SSL tasks into a set of verifiable visual puzzles for RL post-training, requiring neither human nor external model supervision. Training MLLMs on these tasks substantially improves their performance on multimodal understanding and reasoning benchmarks, highlighting the potential of leveraging vision-centric self-supervised tasks for MLLM post-training. We think this work will provide useful experience in devising effective self-supervised verifiable rewards to enable RL at scale. Project page: https://github.com/Jiahao000/SSL-R1.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了SSL-R1，一种通用的自监督强化学习框架，通过将自监督学习任务重新构建为可验证的视觉谜题，直接从图像中推导可验证奖励，用于多模态大语言模型的后训练。
AI.motivation: 现有基于可验证奖励的强化学习方法主要依赖语言先验和昂贵的人工标注，这限制了多模态大语言模型内在视觉理解能力的开发和可扩展的奖励设计，本研究旨在解决这一局限性。
AI.method: 该方法重新审视视觉领域的自监督学习，将广泛使用的自监督学习任务重新构建为一组可验证的视觉谜题，用于强化学习后训练，整个过程无需人类或外部模型监督，实现了完全自监督的奖励生成机制。
AI.result: 在多模态理解和推理基准测试中，使用该方法训练的多模态大语言模型性能显著提升，验证了基于视觉中心自监督任务进行后训练的有效性，展示了自监督可验证奖励在强化学习规模化应用中的潜力。
AI.conclusion: 这项工作为设计有效的自监督可验证奖励提供了有益经验，证明了直接从图像中推导奖励的可行性，为大规模强化学习在多模态大语言模型中的应用开辟了新途径，强调了视觉中心自监督任务在增强模型推理能力方面的重要价值。

title: Render-in-the-Loop: Vector Graphics Generation via Visual Self-Feedback
summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have shown promising capabilities in generating Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) via direct code synthesis. However, existing paradigms typically adopt an open-loop "blind drawing" approach, where models generate symbolic code sequences without perceiving intermediate visual outcomes. This methodology severely underutilizes the powerful visual priors embedded in MLLMs vision encoders, treating SVG generation as a disjointed textual sequence modeling task rather than an integrated visuo-spatial one. Consequently, models struggle to reason about partial canvas states and implicit occlusion relationships, which are visually explicit but textually ambiguous. To bridge this gap, we propose Render-in-the-Loop, a novel generation paradigm that reformulates SVG synthesis as a step-wise, visual-context-aware process. By rendering intermediate code states into a cumulative canvas, the model explicitly observes the evolving visual context at each step, leveraging on-the-fly feedback to guide subsequent generation. However, we demonstrate that applying this visual loop naively to off-the-shelf models is suboptimal due to their inability to leverage incremental visual-code mappings. To address this, we first utilize fine-grained path decomposition to construct dense multi-step visual trajectories, and then introduce a Visual Self-Feedback (VSF) training strategy to condition the next primitive generation on intermediate visual states. Furthermore, a Render-and-Verify (RaV) inference mechanism is proposed to effectively filter degenerate and redundant primitives. Our framework, instantiated on a multimodal foundation model, outperforms strong open-weight baselines on the standard MMSVGBench. This result highlights the remarkable data efficiency and generalization capability of our Render-in-the-Loop paradigm for both Text-to-SVG and Image-to-SVG tasks.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Render-in-the-Loop范式，通过将SVG生成重构为逐步、视觉上下文感知的过程，解决了现有MLLMs在SVG生成中忽视中间视觉结果的局限性，显著提升了生成质量和数据效率。
AI.motivation: 现有多模态大语言模型在生成可缩放矢量图形时通常采用开环的"盲画"方法，模型生成符号代码序列而不感知中间视觉结果，这严重未充分利用MLLMs视觉编码器中嵌入的强大视觉先验，将SVG生成视为离散的文本序列建模任务而非集成的视觉空间任务，导致模型难以推理部分画布状态和隐式遮挡关系。
AI.method: 提出了Render-in-the-Loop生成范式，通过将中间代码状态渲染到累积画布中，使模型在每一步都能显式观察演化的视觉上下文；首先利用细粒度路径分解构建密集多步视觉轨迹，然后引入视觉自反馈训练策略，将下一个基元生成条件化于中间视觉状态；进一步提出渲染验证推理机制以有效过滤退化和冗余基元。
AI.result: 在标准MMSVGBench基准测试中，基于多模态基础模型实例化的框架优于强大的开源基线模型，结果突显了Render-in-the-Loop范式在文本到SVG和图像到SVG任务中具有显著的数据效率和泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 研究证明了将视觉反馈循环整合到SVG生成过程中的重要性，通过显式建模视觉上下文演化，能够更有效地利用MLLMs的视觉先验知识，为符号视觉生成任务提供了新的范式，展示了在有限数据下实现高质量生成的潜力。
